cross_scene_arcs.title: From Legal Warrior to Emotional Survivor
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Miles begins as a committed legal strategist who views divorce as warfare, but his personal entanglement with Marylin exposes the emotional cost of his philosophy. His journey from professional triumph to personal devastation reveals a growing dissonance between his external success and internal emptiness, culminating in the shattering realization that he has become the victim of the very system he mastered.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Miles embraces the role of legal combatant, rejecting compromise in favor of total victory.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Miles is emotionally and professionally undone, realizing he has been manipulated and stripped of control, mirroring the vulnerabilities of his clients.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Miles can rebuild his identity beyond the framework of legal conquest and victimhood remains uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Rise and Fall of Love as Redemption
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Miles's relationship with Marylin evolves from repressed longing to passionate commitment, then collapses into betrayal and manipulation, only to culminate in a fragile reconciliation centered on fatherhood. This arc traces his emotional transformation from detachment to vulnerability, through disillusionment, and toward a tentative redemption rooted in love and responsibility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Miles is emotionally distant, professionally involved in Marylin's prenup, and privately wounded by her engagement to Doyle.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Miles achieves a fragile emotional reconciliation with Marylin, centered on their unborn child, symbolizing a shift from ego-driven love to selfless commitment.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The authenticity of their final connection remains ambiguous—whether it is genuine healing or another cycle of manipulation is left unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Heir Who Inherits Emptiness
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Miles ascends to the pinnacle of professional status—recognized as the firm's true leader—only to reject that status in favor of emotional authenticity. His rise is shadowed by existential dissatisfaction, and his fall from grace becomes a moral reckoning, culminating in public humiliation and the collapse of his social and legal authority.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Miles is formally acknowledged as the successor to Herb Myserson, inheriting institutional power and legacy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Miles is publicly exposed and emotionally broken, his authority undermined by Marylin's legal counterattack and his colleagues' skepticism.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Miles can reclaim status on his own terms—through integrity rather than manipulation—remains an open question.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Betrayal and the Self
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Miles's internal conflict between manipulation and authenticity intensifies as his relationship with Marylin unfolds. Initially believing he is reclaiming love, he gradually becomes the target of a conspiracy he failed to foresee. The external betrayal forces a confrontation with his own complicity in a system of deception, leading to a crisis of identity and moral reckoning.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Miles struggles with repressed feelings for Marylin while maintaining professional control, masking vulnerability with cynicism.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Miles accepts emotional defeat and the death of his idealized love, yet retains a flicker of connection through fatherhood, suggesting partial resolution.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Miles's capacity to trust or love without manipulation—either as victim or perpetrator—remains unproven.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Isolation to Fatherhood: The Final Transformation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Miles's journey from existential isolation to emotional commitment is symbolized by his transformation of the bachelor mansion into a family home and his ultimate sacrifice of personal indulgences for the sake of his unborn child. This arc blends emotional, relational, and symbolic shifts, marking a hard-won evolution from self-destruction to responsibility.

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Reconciliation to Ruin: Rex's Failed Marriage and Financial Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rex's initial desire to reconcile with Marylin to avoid financial collapse evolves into a ruthless legal campaign to retain all assets, culminating in his complete victory but emotional and moral isolation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Rex seeks reconciliation to prevent financial ruin caused by the divorce
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Rex maintains full control over his wealth and legal standing, while Marylin is left financially destitute and emotionally embittered
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The ethical and legal implications of Rex's actions remain unchallenged, leaving questions about justice and accountability unresolved

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Lingering Shadow: Rex's Posthumous Influence on Marylin and Miles
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Even after his death, Rex's unresolved legal legacy—particularly his outdated will—triggers cascading consequences for Marylin and Miles, exposing how past relationships continue to shape present power dynamics and emotional instability.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Rex maintains full control over his wealth and legal standing
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Miles realizes he stands to gain or lose significantly based on his marital connection to Marylin, prompting immediate panic and urgent action
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legal ambiguity of Rex's will and the threat it poses to Miles's autonomy remain unresolved, continuing to drive tension and risk

cross_scene_arcs.title: Power, Performance, and Decline: The Rise and Fall of Rex's Status
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rex transitions from a publicly humiliated spouse to a dominant legal and financial force, then to a flamboyant but physically deteriorating tycoon, ending with his sudden death that exposes the fragility beneath his constructed image of invincibility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Rex is publicly rejected and physically excluded from his home, shifting his focus from reconciliation to survival
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Rex suffers a visible physical collapse and dies of a massive coronary, leaving everything to his daughter Marylin
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The cause and severity of Rex's pain are unknown, leaving his health and ability to maintain control over his environment in question

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unresolved War: Ongoing Conflict Between Rex and Marylin
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: The divorce between Rex and Marylin escalates from personal betrayal to a high-stakes legal battle, with lasting emotional and financial consequences that continue to define Marylin's identity and relationships long after the legal proceedings end.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Rex is publicly rejected and physically excluded from his home
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Marylin acknowledges her past role in Rex's marriage as defined by his prior relationship, reinforcing her awareness of repeating marital patterns
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The truth about Rex's character and the legitimacy of Marylin's intentions remain contested, leaving his public image and private reality in tension

cross_scene_arcs.title: Echoes of the Past: Rex as a Symbol of Transactional Love and Identity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Rex becomes a symbolic figure representing the transactional nature of Marylin's past marriages, invoked repeatedly to contrast emotional authenticity with materialism, and to question the sincerity of relationships built on power and image.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The accumulation of names (Hamilton-Rexroth-Doyle) suggests unresolved questions about Marylin's true identity and the legitimacy of her relationships

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Legal Battle to Emotional Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Marilyn transitions from a legally adversarial stance with Rex to a moment of emotional vulnerability in court, revealing the lingering emotional weight of their relationship despite the divorce.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Moves from defiant confidence to vulnerable composure under legal and emotional pressure
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Emotional conflict between past relationship and current legal battle

cross_scene_arcs.title: Strategic Self-Protection in New Relationships
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Marilyn prioritizes self-preservation in her relationships, first by securing a prenup with Howard and distancing from Miles, then facing emotional isolation and misjudgment from him, culminating in a failed attempt to win his approval through the house renovation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Commits to the Massey prenup and rejects Miles's advance
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Anxious and emotionally destabilized by Miles's reaction
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Uncertainty about the stability of her relationship with Miles

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Power Shift with Miles
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Marilyn's relationship with Miles evolves from hopeful reconciliation to open antagonism, then briefly softens into a moment of influence—revealing the push-pull dynamic of control and connection.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Anxious and emotionally destabilized by Miles's reaction
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Achieves a pivotal moment of connection and influence in her relationship with Miles
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Lingering emotional complexity beneath the power play

cross_scene_arcs.title: Isolation and Misunderstanding
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Marilyn's attempts to seek validation and connection are repeatedly met with judgment and coldness, reinforcing her emotional isolation and pushing her toward a defensive, adversarial stance.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Relationship is now fully antagonistic and legally weaponized
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her reputation and emotional pain are dismissed as manipulation
